summary: The changes across these commits involved adding and updating various documentation files and code notebooks related to large language models (LLMs) and artificial intelligence (AI). Initially, foundational files like a README and attributes file were created. Subsequent commits introduced detailed technical documents on LLM fundamentals, usage, output parameters, and prompt engineering, alongside interactive notebooks for topics such as tokenization, embeddings, attention mechanisms, and transformer heads. Later updates included refinements to these documents and notebooks, as well as new materials like slides, presenter scripts, and safety guidelines for AI systems. Overall, the changes expanded the codebase with educational content and practical resources for understanding and working with LLMs and AI components.
next_steps: Add unit tests to validate functionality in interactive notebooks
Refactor documentation to ensure consistent formatting and style across all technical files
Create a CI/CD pipeline to automatically check for broken links or outdated references in documentation
Enhance safety guidelines by including specific examples of AI risks and mitigation strategies
Optimize slides for readability by incorporating visual aids and reducing text density
